title: aira insularis
intro: Aira insularis, also known as the Channel Island hair grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is primarily found in the Channel Islands, and its rhizomes have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The rhizomes of Aira insularis contain saponins, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and aiding in digestion.
